title: Amy Madigan Wins Best Supporting Actress at 2026 Oscars for 'Weapons'
text: Amy Madigan won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress at the 98th Oscars on March 15, 2026, for her role in Weapons. Madigan's win sets a new record for the longest gap between a first Oscar nomination and a first victory among actresses. Her previous nomination came 40 years ago for Twice in a Lifetime. The race included strong contenders Elle Fanning and Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas for Sentimental Value, Wunmi Mosaku for Sinners, and Teyana Taylor for One Battle After Another. Prior to the ceremony, Madigan had already secured both the Critics Choice and Actor Awards. Upon winning, Madigan expressed genuine emotion, letting out a cathartic scream. She stated she was not expecting this outcome and thanked director Zach Cregger and acknowledged her fellow nominees for their support during awards season.
